sentence.hint: 溢出；氾濫 — 充滿到溢出邊緣
sentence.example: The heavy rain caused the river to overflow its banks.

sentence.hint: 狹窄的；窄小的 — having a short distance from one side to the other
sentence.example: The alley was so narrow that only a very small car could drive along it.

sentence.hint: 程度；限度 — the range or amount of something
sentence.example: Helen was exhausted to such an extent that she fell asleep in the taxi.

sentence.hint: 減少；衰退 — to decrease in size, strength, quality, or value
sentence.example: The president's popularity declined sharply because of his political scandal.

sentence.hint: 突然地；顯著地 — suddenly and in an obvious way
sentence.example: The company's stock price fell dramatically after the new policy.

sentence.hint: 蜂擁；聚集 — to come together or move in large groups
sentence.example: In summer, people flock to the beaches, making them very crowded.

sentence.hint: 擴大；增加 — an increase in size, number, or degree
sentence.example: The expansion of the house took several months to complete.

sentence.hint: 湧入；武裝入侵 — arrival of a large number of people or creatures
sentence.example: Every summer, an invasion of tourists transforms this quiet seaside village.

sentence.hint: 抱怨；投訴 — a negative comment expressing dissatisfaction
sentence.example: Mandy made a complaint to the manager about the rude behavior.

sentence.hint: 粗心的；欠考慮的 — not caring about others' feelings
sentence.example: It was thoughtless of Jim to eat most of the pizza without asking others.

sentence.hint: 房客 — a person who pays to live in someone else's home
sentence.example: Mrs. Jones asked her tenant to move out because he violated the no-pet policy.

sentence.hint: 房東；地主 — someone who owns property rented to others
sentence.example: Brenda signed an agreement with her landlord before moving into the house.

sentence.hint: 偏愛的；不完全的 — liking something very much, OR incomplete
sentence.example: Many people are partial to chocolate ice cream, but Kelly doesn't like it.

sentence.hint: 措施；方法 — an action used to achieve a goal
sentence.example: The government needs to take measures to reduce air pollution.

sentence.hint: 全面的；徹底的 — done very carefully and in great detail
sentence.example: The report was quite thorough; it examined every aspect of the issue.

sentence.hint: 努力；奮鬥 — to try one's best to achieve something
sentence.example: By practicing every day, Alice strove to become the fastest runner at school.

sentence.hint: 壞的；有害的 — not good or causing harm
sentence.example: Chronic overwork will have a negative impact on one's health.

sentence.hint: 過度旅遊 — too many tourists overrunning a place
sentence.example: Barcelona is suffering from a phenomenon called overtourism.

transform.original: The alarm will only go off if an earthquake is detected.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only if an earthquake is detected will the alarm go off.

transform.original: We can only see this kind of insect in the Amazon rainforest.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only in the Amazon rainforest can we see this kind of insect.

transform.original: The castle built on the rocky island is only accessible by boat.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only by boat is the castle built on the rocky island accessible.

transform.original: Muslim women can only enter a mosque with all their skin covered.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only with all their skin covered can Muslim women enter a mosque.

transform.original: Citizens of this country are allowed to vote only after they turn eighteen.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only after they turn eighteen are citizens of this country allowed to vote.

transform.original: You can only cross the street when the traffic light turns green.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only when the traffic light turns green can you cross the street.

transform.original: Orchid Island is sixteen times larger than Turtle Island.
transform.instruction: 改為 'times as ... as ...' 倍數句型
transform.answer: Orchid Island is sixteen times as large as Turtle Island.

transform.original: Imported apples cost three times more money than domestic ones.
transform.instruction: 改為 'times as ... as ...' 倍數句型
transform.answer: Imported apples cost three times as much money as domestic ones.

transform.original: The cheetah runs twice faster than a human being.
transform.instruction: 改為 'times as ... as ...' 倍數句型
transform.answer: The cheetah can run twice as fast as a human being.

transform.original: Mark behaves himself when his parents are around.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only when 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only when Mark's parents are around does he behave himself.

transform.original: The impact of overtourism can be reduced by introducing practical measures.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only by 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only by introducing practical measures can the impact of overtourism be reduced.

transform.original: Overtourism will be manageable after the authorities develop sustainable solutions.
transform.instruction: 改為 Only after 開頭的倒裝句
transform.answer: Only after the authorities have developed sustainable solutions will overtourism be manageable.

translation.source: 巴塞隆納正在遭受一種叫做「過度旅遊」的現象。
translation.answer: Barcelona is suffering from a phenomenon called "overtourism."

translation.source: 居民們受夠了一些遊客不適當的行為。
translation.answer: Residents are fed up with some visitors' inappropriate behavior.

translation.source: 政府提出了一個全面的計畫。
translation.answer: The government has come up with a thorough plan.

translation.source: 只有透過引進實際措施，過度旅遊的影響才能被減少。
translation.answer: Only by introducing practical measures can the impact of overtourism be reduced.

translation.source: 1992 年奧運讓巴塞隆納登上了旅遊地圖。
translation.answer: The 1992 Olympics put Barcelona on the tourist map.

translation.source: 城市官員正試圖在當地社區與國際遊客之間取得平衡。
translation.answer: City officials are striving to strike a balance between the local community and international visitors.

translation.source: The number of annual visitors to Barcelona grew to almost five times as large as that in 1992.
translation.answer: 巴塞隆納每年的遊客人數成長到幾乎是 1992 年的五倍。

translation.source: This invasion of visitors has resulted in various problems.
translation.answer: 這波遊客的湧入造成了各式各樣的問題。

translation.source: Only after the authorities have developed sustainable solutions will overtourism be manageable.
translation.answer: 只有在當局發展出永續的解決方案之後，過度旅遊才能被控制。

translation.source: Local hotels and businesses benefited from this expansion of tourism.
translation.answer: 當地的旅館與商家從觀光業的擴張中受益。

translation.source: Landlords are partial to renting their places out through Airbnb to make more money.
translation.answer: 房東們偏好透過 Airbnb 出租房屋以賺取更多錢。

vocabulary.question: Tourists ______ to Barcelona every summer, making the streets very crowded.
vocabulary.explanation: flock (蜂擁) — 大量遊客成群湧入的最佳動詞。

vocabulary.question: The city's quality of life has ______ dramatically due to overtourism.
vocabulary.explanation: decline (衰退) — 生活品質下降。

vocabulary.question: The local government took practical ______ to limit the number of tourists.
vocabulary.explanation: measures (措施) — take measures 採取措施。

vocabulary.question: The ______ asked his tenants to keep the noise down at night.
vocabulary.explanation: landlord (房東) 對應 tenant (房客)。

vocabulary.question: Many residents are fed up ______ visitors' inappropriate behavior.
vocabulary.explanation: be fed up with (受夠了) — 固定介系詞 with。

vocabulary.question: The careless waiter caused the glass to ______, making the table wet.
Mandy is ______ to chocolate, but her sister prefers vanilla.
vocabulary.explanation: be partial to (偏愛) = like very much。

vocabulary.question: The doctor explained the ______ of the patient's injuries.
vocabulary.explanation: extent (程度) — the extent of injuries 傷勢嚴重程度。

vocabulary.question: Alice ______ to become the fastest runner by practicing every day.
Chronic overwork will have a ______ impact on one's health.
It was very ______ of Jim to eat all the pizza without asking the others.
vocabulary.explanation: thoughtless (粗心)，反義詞為 thoughtful (體貼)。

vocabulary.question: Ron passed the test and finally got his driver's ______.
vocabulary.explanation: license (執照) — driver's license 駕照。

vocabulary.question: Barry has many ______ when choosing which university to attend.
vocabulary.explanation: option (選擇)，許多選擇 = many options。

vocabulary.question: The report was very ______; it examined every detail of the issue.
Barcelona receives more than fifteen times ______ many visitors ______ its actual population.
vocabulary.explanation: 倍數句型：N times as + adj/adv + as。

vocabulary.question: Only by introducing practical measures ______ the impact of overtourism be reduced.
vocabulary.explanation: Only + 副詞片語在句首 → 主詞與助動詞倒裝。

vocabulary.question: The 1992 Olympics put Barcelona on the tourist ______.
vocabulary.explanation: put... on the map (使...為人所知) — 慣用語。

vocabulary.question: Tourism is often described as a double-edged ______.
vocabulary.explanation: double-edged sword (雙面刃) — 課文標題隱喻。
